calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Oct-28-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RICHARD J. COLLIER OF COOPER WHITE & COOPER, TEL. NO. 765-6220 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONERS;
ATTY. PAMELA E. SMITH, TEL. NO. (408) 396-6910 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE REAL PARTY IN INTEREST;
ATTY. RORY J. COFFEY, TEL. NO. (916) 795-3938 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONERS STEPHEN WILSON, WENDY WILSON, DAVID WILSON AND PETER WILSON's Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus is Denied. All of Respondent's findings were supported by the weight of the evidence. The statutes governing beneficiary designations should be "construed to effectuate the employee's intent as to whom his beneficiary should be." (Coughlin v. Board of Administration (1984) 152 Cal.App.3d 70, 72; see also Watenpaugh v. State Teachers Retirement ((1959) 51 Cal.2d 675.).) The Beneficiary Designation executed by Dr. Wilson indicates his intent for Mr. Konou to be the beneficiary of his CalPERS death benefits. Although the Beneficiary Designation was statutorily revoked, it remains valid evidence of Dr. Wilson's intent and serves as a written modification of the 2006 Domestic Partnership Agreement under Sections 14 and 19.1. Petitioners have not met their burden of showing by clear and convincing evidence that Mr. Konou knowingly and intelligently waived his right to the CalPERS death benefits, as required by Hittle v. Santa Barbara County Employees Retirement Association. ((1985) 39 Cal.3d 374.) The evidence shows that once Mr. Konou was fully apprised of the financial information pertaining to the benefits, and therefore was in a position to knowingly and intelligently waive his right, he chose not to do so. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)
